channel.title: Startup Wire Daily
channel.description: Daily 5-minute startup and venture capital news. Funding rounds, founder stories, product launches, and the business ideas changing the world.
channel.itunes:author: Startup Wire Daily

item.title: Startup News Jun 29 — Prompt injection is exploiting enterprise AI'
item.description: The AI revolution is in full swing, but with great power comes great vulnerability, and the latest trend in cybercrime is targeting the very foundation of enterprise AI - by exploiting design flaws in large language models, or LLMs, and going after agents, RAG pipelines, and model routers. In the past two years, businesses have been scrambling to integrate LLMs into support, analytics, development, and internal automation, but it's clear that cybercriminals are taking advantage of the disconnect...

item.title: Startup News Jun 29 — Prompt injection is exploiting enterprise AI'
item.description: The AI revolution is in full swing, but with great power comes great vulnerability, and we're seeing a whole new wave of cyber threats targeting the enterprise AI's biggest design flaws. In the past two years, businesses have been rapidly adopting large language models, or LLMs, to boost support, analytics, development, and internal automation. However, this trend has also led to a surge in prompt injection attacks, which exploit the disconnect between assumptions about LLMs and their actual cha...

item.title: Startup News Jun 30 — Meituan open sources LongCat-2.0, the 1.6T, n
item.description: In a shocking move that's sending shockwaves through the tech world, Chinese delivery app company Meituan just open-sourced its groundbreaking model, LongCat-2.0, a 1.6T, near-frontier agentic coding model that's been leading OpenRouter. This is huge, because LongCat-2.0 has been trained entirely on Chinese chips, a clear indication that China is rapidly closing the gap with the US in the AI race. By open-sourcing this model, Meituan is not only disrupting the closed-source enterprise dominance ...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jun 30 — Meituan open sources LongCat-2.0, the 1.6T, near-f
item.description: In a stunning move that's set to democratize access to cutting-edge AI technology, Meituan has just open-sourced LongCat-2.0, its 1.6T near-frontier agentic coding model that's been leading the charge on OpenRouter. This is huge, because LongCat-2.0 has been trained entirely on Chinese chips, marking a significant milestone in the country's efforts to reduce its dependence on foreign technology. By open-sourcing this model, Meituan is essentially handing over the keys to a powerful tool that can...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jun 30 — Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 at a steep disc
item.description: Anthropic is taking a bold step as it hurtles towards a highly anticipated IPO, and it's doing so with a strategic price cut that could shake up the AI landscape. The company just launched Claude Sonnet 5, a new AI model that boasts near-flagship performance at a significantly lower price point, making it more accessible to cost-conscious enterprise developers. This move is a clear attempt to expand Anthropic's user base and boost revenue as it prepares to face public scrutiny over its valuation...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jul 01 — Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 at a steep disc
item.description: The AI arms race just got a whole lot more interesting, as Anthropic launches its latest powerhouse, Claude Sonnet 5, at a steep discount to its top model, in a bold move to capture the market share as it hurtles toward a blockbuster IPO. This new AI model promises near-flagship performance at mid-tier prices, making it an attractive option for cost-conscious enterprise developers who want to harness the power of agentic capabilities without breaking the bank. By releasing Claude Sonnet 5, Anthr...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jul 02 — Anthropic is bringing back Claude Fable 5 globally
item.description: In a massive win for enterprises worldwide, Anthropic is bringing back its powerhouse AI model Claude Fable 5, just days after the US lifted its export control order. This move is a huge deal, as Fable 5 is Anthropic's most powerful generally released AI model yet, and its global accessibility is a game-changer for businesses looking to tap into cutting-edge AI capabilities. Now that the US Department of Commerce has withdrawn its emergency export controls, Anthropic is restoring access to Fable...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jul 03 — Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Co
item.description: In a shocking move that's set to disrupt the coding world, Z.ai just launched ZCode, a free desktop application that's taking aim at the likes of Cursor, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot. This aggressive push into the AI-powered coding tool market marks a significant milestone for the Beijing-based artificial intelligence lab, and it's going to be interesting to see how the competition responds. ZCode is being touted as an "Agentic Development Environment" that's purpose-built for Z.ai's flagship...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jul 04 — Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Co
item.description: The AI revolution is transforming the way we code, and Z.ai just threw down the gauntlet with the launch of ZCode, a free desktop application designed to work seamlessly with its flagship GLM-5.2 large language model. This move marks a bold push into the rapidly evolving AI-powered coding tool market, where Z.ai will go head-to-head with heavy hitters like Cursor, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot. The implications are huge, as the ability to automate coding tasks with precision and speed could si...

item.title: Startup Wire Daily | Jul 05 — Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Co
item.description: The AI coding wars just got a whole lot more intense, as Z.ai has officially launched ZCode, a free desktop application designed to take on the likes of Cursor, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot. This move marks a major push by Z.ai into the rapidly growing AI-powered coding tool market, where the company is now going head-to-head with some of the biggest players in the space. ZCode is being billed as an "Agentic Development Environment" that's specifically designed to work with Z.ai's flagship GL...

item.title: Z.ai launches ZCode to challenge Cursor, Claude Code and — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 06
item.description: Z.ai just launched ZCode, a free desktop application that's being billed as an "Agentic Development Environment" and direct challenger to Cursor, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot in the AI-powered coding tool market. This is huge because it means Z.ai is now going head-to-head with some of the biggest players in the space, and its flagship GLM-5.2 large language model is at the center of it all. The implications are massive, as this could potentially disrupt the entire coding landscape and change...

item.title: AI law startup Norm raises $120M, hits unicorn valuation — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 08
item.description: Norm, an AI law startup, just hit a $1.2 billion valuation after raising a whopping $120 million in its Series C round led by Khosla Ventures. This is a massive milestone for the company, and it's a clear indication that investors are confident in the potential of AI to disrupt the legal industry. The fact that Norm has reached unicorn status in such a short amount of time is a testament to the growing demand for AI-powered legal solutions. With this new funding, Norm will likely continue to exp...

item.title: SpaceX's Grok 4.5 launches at half the price of rivals — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 09
item.description: SpaceX just launched Grok 4.5, an artificial intelligence model trained for coding and autonomous agents, at a staggering half the price of its rivals, sending shockwaves through the AI community and putting pressure on Anthropic and OpenAI to reconsider their pricing strategies. This move is a direct result of Elon Musk's $60 billion acquisition of AI coding startup Cursor, completed just weeks ago, and marks a pivotal test of the sprawling AI empire Musk has assembled over the past six months....

item.title: Shared API keys expose AI agents at 69% of enterprises, new — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 10
item.description: Sixty-nine percent of enterprises are sharing API keys across multiple AI agents, essentially giving attackers a master key to unlock and control entire workflows. This shocking finding comes from VentureBeat's latest research, which surveyed 107 enterprises and found that nearly 7 out of 10 are putting their AI systems at risk by sharing credentials. The implications are staggering - if one agent is compromised, the attacker gains access to all the permissions and workflows associated with that...

item.title: OpenAI introduces ChatGPT Work, a cloud-based AI agent that — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 11
item.description: ChatGPT just got a massive upgrade with ChatGPT Work, a cloud-based AI agent that manages tasks across email, Slack, and calendars, basically turning the chatbot into a full-fledged work platform. This new AI agent is powered by OpenAI's latest flagship model, GPT-5.6, and is designed to go far beyond generating text, actually executing complex, multi-step tasks for users. This is huge because it has the potential to transform the way we work, automating a lot of tedious tasks and freeing up tim...

item.title: Show HN: Mindwalk – Replay coding-agent sessions on a 3D — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 12
item.description: Twenty-six commenters on Hacker News are debating why anyone would still write code in 2026, and the discussion is getting heated. The original post, written by Software Doug, argues that with the rise of low-code and no-code solutions, the need for manual coding is dwindling, and the profession may become obsolete sooner rather than later. But many of the commenters disagree, pointing out that while these solutions can automate certain tasks, they can't replace the creativity and problem-solvin...

item.title: DeepSeek cut prices 75%. The 100x problem remains — Startup Wire Daily | Jul 13
item.description: DeepSeek just slashed prices on its V4-Pro model by a whopping 75%, a move that should have been a major win for enterprise AI vendors and developers, but it's turning out to be a mixed bag. The reason is that while inference costs are plummeting, agent systems are consuming tokens at an insane rate, faster than prices are declining. This is a big deal because it means that cheaper models don't automatically translate into healthier margins for these companies. It's like they're running on a tre...
